commit.message:
feat: send PLAYBACK_STARTED/ENDED events to CMS (#94)

* feat: send PLAYBACK_STARTED/ENDED events to CMS on state transitions

Device now immediately sends PLAYBACK_STARTED and PLAYBACK_ENDED WebSocket
messages when playback state changes, replacing the scheduler-driven approach.
This enables near-instant 'Now Playing' updates on the CMS dashboard.

Changes:
- Track current schedule_id/name/asset in CMSClient
- Send PLAYBACK_STARTED when a new schedule starts playing
- Send PLAYBACK_ENDED when playback stops or a different schedule takes over
- Fire-and-forget pattern: events silently dropped if WS disconnected
- Fix test fixtures to include new tracking attributes
- Fix asyncio.get_event_loop() deprecation in loop_count tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: immediate PLAYBACK_ENDED via player watch loop

Add a lightweight _player_watch_loop (2s poll of current.json) that
detects when the player transitions from 'play' to another mode (e.g.
after loop_count EOS).  When detected, it wakes the eval loop via an
asyncio.Event so PLAYBACK_ENDED is sent within ~2s instead of waiting
for the full 15s EVAL_INTERVAL.

Changes:
- Add _eval_wake Event and _last_player_mode tracker to CMSClient
- Add _player_watch_loop coroutine (PLAYER_WATCH_INTERVAL=2s)
- Replace asyncio.sleep(EVAL_INTERVAL) with wait_for(_eval_wake)
- Add 3 tests for player watch mode transition detection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: version bump and test for fire-and-forget fetch_request

- Bump version 1.6.0 → 1.7.0
- Fix test_missing_asset_requests_fetch: wrap _evaluate_schedule in
  asyncio.run() so the fire-and-forget create_task is flushed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add current_state_path to _Settings in reconnect test

The _player_watch_loop reads settings.current_state_path, which the
test's _Settings mock was missing.

commit.message:
fix: resolve caching issues in profile access control (#66)

* fix: remove exact version tag from scheduled daily builds

Scheduled daily builds were incorrectly being tagged with the full
version number (e.g., 2.0.7). Daily builds should only receive:
- latest
- short SHA
- major version (e.g., 2)
- major.minor version (e.g., 2.0)

The exact version tag (e.g., 2.0.7) should only be applied on:
- Pushes to main branch
- Manual workflow_dispatch runs
- Git tag pushes (via semver patterns)

This change removes 'github.event_name == schedule' from the exact
version tag condition while keeping it for the floating tags.

* fix: resolve caching issues in profile access control

Split into separate build + release pipelines for RM

pipelines/build.yml:
  - Builds container images, pushes to ACR
  - failure_scenario parameter injects bugs at build time
  - Triggered on push to main (src/* or bugs/*)

pipelines/release.yml:
  - Deploys images from ACR to Container Apps + App Service
  - buildId parameter selects which image tag to deploy
  - Validates health after deployment
  - Triggered manually or chained from build

SRE Agent Release Management can now track these separately:
  Build pipeline → BuildSucceeded/BuildFailed triggers
  Release pipeline → can be triggered by SRE Agent

commit.message:
fix: parse session time and tokens from new CLI stats format (#83)

## Problem

PR #82 shows stats as `💰 1 premium · 🧠 high` — missing elapsed time (⏱️)
and token usage (🤖/📊).

The Copilot CLI changed its stats output format and we only fixed the
premium requests regex, not the time/model lines:

| Field | Old format | New format |
|-------|-----------|------------|
| Premium | `Total usage est: 3 Premium` | `Requests 1 Premium (15m
44s)` |
| Time | `Total session time: 15m 44s` | Embedded in Requests line above
|
| API time | `API time spent: 6m 29s` | Gone |
| Tokens | `gpt-5.4 5.5m in, 34.0k out` | `Tokens ↑ 5.5m • ↓ 34.0k •
5.3m (cached)` |

## Changes

- `_parse_stats()`: extract `session_time` from parenthesized time on
the Requests line, capture `tokens_line` from new Tokens line
- `CLIResult`: added `tokens_line` field
- `_format_stage_stats()`: shows 📊 tokens_line when old-format model
breakdown isn't available
- `review_pr()`: passes `tokens_line` through result dict

**Expected output after fix:**
```
💰 1 premium · ⏱️ 6m 16s · 🧠 high · 📊 ↑ 1.1m • ↓ 18.9k • 1.0m (cached) • 11.7k (reasoning)
```

150 tests pass (4 new).

commit.message:
fix: stabilize kind nightly EPS for saturation-target lanes (#250)

- Increase collector daemonset rollout timeout from 120s to 300s in
  bench/kind/run.py.  The 120s limit was intermittently hit by all three
  collectors at eps_per_pod=1_000_000 (t1m) when GitHub Actions runners
  were slow, causing 6 of 48 lanes to fail with 'timed out waiting for
  the condition' before the benchmark even started.

- Add is_saturation_target() helper to render_issue_summary.py.  Lanes
  at eps_per_pod >= 100_000 (or unbounded max) are saturation probes;
  their failures indicate throughput-limiting, not correctness regression.

- Exclude saturation-target failures from the gating verdict:
  - Suite PASS/FAIL is now driven by gating_failed_count (non-saturation
    failures only).  The full failed_count is still reported for
    visibility.
  - Integrity Alerts table shows 'THROUGHPUT-LIMITED' instead of 'FAIL'
    for saturation-mode lanes.
  - New 'Throughput-Limited Probes (non-gating)' section replaces the
    old 'Failing Benchmarks' entry for these lanes, with an explanatory
    note so readers understand the framing.
  - JSON payload gains gating_failed_count field.

- Update upsert_issue.sh to use gating_failed_count when present, so
  the issue title ([PASS]/[FAIL]) and detail string reflect only gating
  failures.  The issue detail shows non-gating count separately when
  non-zero (e.g. '6 non-gating, 42 passing').

fix: countdown timer flicker and accuracy (#173)

* fix: countdown timer flicker and accuracy

Server now renders 'Ns' directly when ≤30s remaining instead of
'less than a minute', eliminating the flip-flop between server-rendered
text and JS countdown. JS tick() runs immediately on page load instead
of waiting 1s for the first setInterval callback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: dashboard live countdown and seconds in activity timestamps

Replace full page reload with in-place DOM updates for the Now Playing
countdown timer. The 5s poll now syncs remaining_seconds from the API
and a 1s ticker interpolates between polls, so the countdown never
resets. Structural changes (new schedules, device status, activity)
still trigger a full reload.

Add seconds to Recent Activity and History timestamps so short clips
no longer appear to start and end at the same time.

Bump version to 1.17.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: recompute remaining_seconds fresh in API to prevent countdown jumps

The scheduler tick runs every 15s, so remaining_seconds in _now_playing
can be up to 15s stale. The dashboard JS polls every 5s and syncs from
this value, causing the countdown to jump back to a higher number.

Fix: store start_time_raw and end_time_raw in _now_playing, then
recompute remaining_seconds at request time in dashboard_json()
using the current server time. This gives the JS countdown a fresh
baseline on every poll.

Also bumps version to 1.17.6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: fresh remaining_seconds on page render + reduce reload triggers

Two root causes of countdown jumping:

1. Initial page render used stale remaining_seconds from the scheduler
   (15s tick). Now recomputes fresh in both dashboard() and
   dashboard_json().

2. Structural fingerprint was too sensitive — activity_count and
   starting/mismatch status flags caused unnecessary page reloads
   that reset the JS countdown. Removed transient fields from the
   fingerprint so reloads only happen for truly structural changes
   (different schedules, device list, pending/orphaned/offline).

Also adds a past-time-window warning when saving a schedule whose
end date is today but end time has already passed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add seconds to data-utc timestamp formatter in app.js

The client-side JS in app.js overwrites all [data-utc] elements with
toLocaleTimeString using only hour+minute, stripping the seconds that
the server-side Jinja2 template renders. Adding second: '2-digit' to
the formatter options fixes the display.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore starting/mismatch status in structural fingerprint

The starting and mismatch flags need to trigger reloads so the
badge transitions are visible (Starting -> Playing, mismatch
detection). These cause at most one reload per transition, unlike
activity_count which caused repeated reloads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: match number input height to time input height

Both input[type=time] and input[type=number] now use 0.6rem vertical
padding so loop count and priority fields align with start/end time.

commit.message:
Reframe as Meeting Planner — merge tabs, meeting-first hierarchy

Core reframe: Meeting Planner is the #1 USP, not World Clock.

- Merge World Clock + Meeting Planner into one unified tab
- Meeting planner flows as Step 1 (add cities) → Step 2 (find time)
- Clock cards show as context between steps (current times)
- Local time moved to bottom (compact, supportive)
- Tab renamed: '📅 Meeting Planner' (was '🕐 World Clock')
- 3 tabs now (was 4): Meeting Planner, Convert Time, DST Guide
- Hero title: 'Global Meeting Planner & World Clock'
- Team presets now load into shared clock list
- All city add/remove actions sync both clocks and timeline
- Updated SEO title, description, FAQ, JSON-LD schema
- Step numbered headers (① Add cities → ② Find time)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
